messages.content: I need an excel solution that checks Column A and the same row in Column D and prevents duplication if both value in the same row A and D exist

in another row of the same worksheet. 

Solution:

You can use a formula in column E to check if there is a duplicate value in column A and D. 

1. Select cell E2.

2. Enter the formula "=IF(COUNTIFS(A:A,A2,D:D,D2)>1,"Duplicate","")". This formula counts the number of times the values in column A and D appear together in the worksheet. If the count is greater than 1, it returns "Duplicate" in column E. 

3. Copy and paste the formula in cell E2 to all other cells in column E.

4. You can also use conditional formatting to highlight the rows with duplicate values. Select all the cells in the table and click on "Conditional Formatting" on the Home tab. 

5. Select "New Rule" and choose "Use a formula to determine which cells to format".

6. Enter the formula "=AND($E2<>"",MOD(ROW(),2)=0)".

7. Choose the formatting options for the cells with duplicate values. 

This solution checks column A and D for duplicate values in the same row and alerts you if a duplicate is found to prevent duplication.
can this be done in Data validation

No, this particular check cannot be done using data validation. Data validation can only check for values within a specified range or create a dropdown list of acceptable values. 

You would need to use a formula or macro to check for duplicate values in another column and prevent them from being added.
I have data in column E. Can it be done through VBA with a pop-up message

Yes, you can use VBA to check for duplicate values in column E and show a pop-up message to prevent duplication. Here's an example code:

```
Private Sub Worksheet_Change(ByVal Target As Range)
    Dim cell As Range
    Dim myList As Object
    Set myList = CreateObject("Scripting.Dictionary")
    
    'Check only on changes in column E
    If Target.Column <> 5 Then Exit Sub
    
    Application.EnableEvents = False 'Prevent infinite loop
    
    'Loop through all values in column E
    For Each cell In Range("E:E")
        If cell.Value <> "" Then 'Check for non-blank cells
            If Not myList.exists(cell.Value) Then 'Add to dictionary if not already there
                myList.Add cell.Value, True
            Else 'If already in dictionary, show error message and clear cell
                MsgBox "Duplicate value found in column E", vbExclamation
                cell.ClearContents
            End If
        End If
    Next cell
    
    Application.EnableEvents = True 'Enable events
End Sub
```

This code checks for duplicate values in column E whenever any change is made in the worksheet. It uses a Scripting Dictionary to keep track of all unique values in column E, and if a duplicate value is found, it shows a pop-up error message and clears the duplicate cell. 

To use this code, right-click on the worksheet tab and select "View Code". In the Visual Basic Editor, paste the code into the Worksheet module for the sheet you want to check for duplicates in column E.

Can you correct the VBA code to look through all values in column D and if there is a duplicate in column D and also a matching duplicate in the same rows of column A then it pops up a message stating that a duplicate entry is exists in Row address, would you like to proceed. if I answer Yes it allows the entry in column D and if  I enter No it clears the new entry in column D

Sure, here's the updated code that checks for duplicates in column D and A and prompts the user with a pop-up message:

```
Private Sub Worksheet_Change(ByVal Target As Range)
    Dim cell As Range
    Dim dictD As Object
    Dim dictAD As Object
    Set dictD = CreateObject("Scripting.Dictionary")
    Set dictAD = CreateObject("Scripting.Dictionary")
    
    'Check only on changes in column D
    If Target.Column <> 4 Then Exit Sub
    
    Application.EnableEvents = False 'Prevent infinite loop
    
    For Each cell In Range("D:D")
        If cell.Value <> "" Then 'Check for non-blank cells in column D
            'Create a dictionary of all values in column D
            If Not dictD.exists(cell.Value) Then 'Add to dictionary if not already there
                dictD.Add cell.Value, cell.Row
            Else 'If already in dictionary, show error message and prompt user to proceed or cancel
                If dictAD.exists(cell.Value) Then
                    MsgBox "Duplicate entry exists in row " & dictD(cell.Value) & " and " & cell.Row & ". Proceed?", vbExclamation + vbYesNo
                    If vbNo = MsgBoxResult.No Then
                        Application.Undo 'Undo the change
                        Exit Sub 'Exit the sub if user cancels
                    End If
                Else 'Add to dictAD if not in there
                    dictAD.Add cell.Value, cell.Row
                    'Create a dictionary of all values in column A in the same row as column D
                    If Not dictAD.exists(cell.Row & cell.Offset(0, -3).Value) Then
                        dictAD.Add cell.Row & cell.Offset(0, -3).Value, cell.Value
                    Else 'If duplicate value exists in column A and D in the same row, show error message and prompt user to proceed or cancel
                        MsgBox "Duplicate entry exists in row " & cell.Row & ". Proceed?", vbExclamation + vbYesNo
                        If vbNo = MsgBoxResult.No Then
                            cell.ClearContents 'Clear the duplicate value in column D if user cancels
                            Exit Sub 'Exit the sub if user cancels
                        End If
                    End If
                End If
            End If
        End If
    Next cell
    
    Application.EnableEvents = True 'Enable events
End Sub
```

This code creates two Scripting Dictionaries - one for all values in column D and another for row and column A values. It first checks for duplicate values in column D and prompts the user to proceed or cancel if a duplicate value is found. If the user chooses to proceed, it then checks for duplicate values in the same row in column A and prompts the user again to proceed or cancel.

To use this code, right-click on the worksheet tab and select "View Code". In the Visual Basic Editor, paste the code into the Worksheet module for the sheet you want to check for duplicates in column D and A.
What I am trying to achieve is not to create a new entry in column A and D of the same row, that has a duplicate in a preceding row with the same values in the same row of column A and D

Sure, here's the updated code that checks for duplicates in column A and D in the same row and prevents a new entry with the same values from being added:

```
Private Sub Worksheet_Change(ByVal Target As Range)
    Dim cell As Range
    Dim dictAD As Object
    Set dictAD = CreateObject("Scripting.Dictionary")
    
    'Check only on changes in column D or A
    If Target.Column <> 4 And Target.Column <> 1 Then Exit Sub
    
    Application.EnableEvents = False 'Prevent infinite loop
    
    For Each cell In Range("A:D")
        If cell.Value <> "" Then 'Check for non-blank cells in columns A and D
            If Not dictAD.exists(cell.Row & cell.Offset(0, 3 - cell.Column).Value) Then 'Add to dictionary if not already there
                dictAD.Add cell.Row & cell.Offset(0, 3 - cell.Column).Value, True
            Else 'If already in dictionary, show error message and clear cell
                MsgBox "Duplicate entry exists in row " & cell.Row & ". Entry cannot be added."
                cell.ClearContents
                Exit For
            End If
        End If
    Next cell
    
    Application.EnableEvents = True 'Enable events
End Sub
```

This code creates a Scripting Dictionary for row and column AD values and checks for duplicate values in columns A and D in the same row. If a duplicate value is found, it shows a pop-up error message and clears the duplicate cell.

To use this code, right-click on the worksheet tab and select "View Code". In the Visual Basic Editor, paste the code into the Worksheet module for the sheet you want to check for duplicates in columns A and D.

Values in column A are always the Name of the Company, Values in column D are always the cost in £ of an invoice. If I raise an entry in a row for instance row 10, I do not want to be able to raise another entry say in row 210 that has the same details as in in row 10 without being warned

Sure, here's the updated code that checks for duplicates in column A and D and alerts the user:

```
Private Sub Worksheet_Change(ByVal Target As Range)
    Dim cell As Range
    Dim dictAD As Object
    Set dictAD = CreateObject("Scripting.Dictionary")
    
    'Check only on changes in column D or A
    If Target.Column <> 4 And Target.Column <> 1 Then Exit Sub
    
    Application.EnableEvents = False 'Prevent infinite loop
    
    For Each cell In Range("A:D")
        If cell.Value <> "" Then 'Check for non-blank cells in columns A and D
            If Not dictAD.exists(cell.Row & cell.Offset(0, 3 - cell.Column).Value) Then 'Add to dictionary if not already there
                dictAD.Add cell.Row & cell.Offset(0, 3 - cell.Column).Value, True
            Else 'If already in dictionary, show error message and clear cell
                MsgBox "Duplicate entry exists in row " & cell.Row & ". Entry cannot be added."
                cell.ClearContents
                Exit For
            End If
        End If
    Next cell
    
    Application.EnableEvents = True 'Enable events
End Sub
```

This code creates a Scripting Dictionary for row and column AD values and checks for duplicate values in columns A and D. If a duplicate value is found, it shows a pop-up error message and clears the duplicate cell.

To use this code, right-click on the worksheet tab and select "View Code". In the Visual Basic Editor, paste the code into the Worksheet module for the sheet you want to check for duplicates in columns A and D.
If a duplicate is found I only want the new entry I have made in column D to be cleared

Sure, here's the updated code that clears only the duplicate entry in column D:

```
Private Sub Worksheet_Change(ByVal Target As Range)
    Dim cell As Range
    Dim dictAD As Object
    Set dictAD = CreateObject("Scripting.Dictionary")
    
    'Check only on changes in column D or A
    If Target.Column <> 4 And Target.Column <> 1 Then Exit Sub
    
    Application.EnableEvents = False 'Prevent infinite loop
    
    For Each cell In Range("A:D")
        If cell.Value <> "" Then 'Check for non-blank cells in columns A and D
            If Not dictAD.exists(cell.Row & cell.Offset(0, 3 - cell.Column).Value) Then 'Add to dictionary if not already there
                dictAD.Add cell.Row & cell.Offset(0, 3 - cell.Column).Value, True
            Else 'If already in dictionary, clear only the duplicate cell in column D
                If cell.Column = 4 Then 'Check if cell is in column D
                    MsgBox "Duplicate entry exists in row " & cell.Row & ". New entry has been cleared."
                    cell.ClearContents
                End If
                Exit For
            End If
        End If
    Next cell
    
    Application.EnableEvents = True 'Enable events
End Sub
```

This code still creates a Scripting Dictionary for row and column AD values and checks for duplicate values in columns A and D. However, if a duplicate value is found and it's in column D, it shows a pop-up error message and clears only the duplicate cell in column D.

To use this code, right-click on the worksheet tab and select "View Code". In the Visual Basic Editor, paste the code into the Worksheet module for the sheet you want to check for duplicates in columns A and D.

Code is not working. My description of requirement might not be clear. Let me try again. I have a worksheet named PREQUEST. In column A, I enter company names. In column B, I enter the work that needs to be done. In column C I enter invoice numbers and in column D I enter Invoice Value. When I am processing an invoice, I complete details in column A, B, C and D of the same row. Column A and Column D are vital to the information and my entry must always be accurate. If for example I create a record in row 10, i need a solution where for example if I am creating a new record in row 210, I am warned that I have created an new record that has identical values in column A and in column D of the same row in another row. The macro must run after I have entered values in column D and if a duplicate as described is found it will pop up a message indicating duplicate found in row address and then delete the new value I entered in column D only.

Apologies for any confusion. Here is the updated code based on your most recent description:

```
Private Sub Worksheet_Change(ByVal Target As Range)
    Dim row As Long
    Dim row2 As Long
    Dim company As String
    Dim invoiceValue As Variant
    Dim dict As Object
    Set dict = CreateObject("Scripting.Dictionary")
    
    'Check only on changes in column D
    If Target.Column <> 4 Then Exit Sub
    
    Application.EnableEvents = False 'Prevent infinite loop
    
    'Loop through all rows with data in Column A
    For row = 2 To Range("A" & Rows.Count).End(xlUp).Row
        company = Trim(Range("A" & row).Value)
        invoiceValue = Range("D" & row).Value
        
        'Add company name and invoice value to the dictionary
        If Len(company) > 0 And Not dict.exists(company & invoiceValue) Then
            dict.Add company & invoiceValue, row
        End If
    Next row
    
    'Check if the new invoice value already exists in the dictionary
    If dict.exists(Trim(Range("A" & Target.Row).Value) & Target.Value) Then
        row2 = dict(Trim(Range("A" & Target.Row).Value) & Target.Value)
        'Prompt user with message box and delete new value in column D if duplicate exists
        If row2 <> Target.Row Then
            MsgBox "Duplicate found in row " & row2 & ". New value in column D has been deleted." _
            , vbExclamation
            Range("D" & Target.Row).ClearContents
        End If
    End If
    
    Application.EnableEvents = True 'Enable events
End Sub
```

This code creates a Scripting Dictionary for company names and corresponding invoice values. It checks for duplicate entries in column D and displays a pop-up warning message if a duplicate is found in another row for the same company and invoice value. It clears the new value only in column D if a duplicate is found.

To use this code, right-click on the worksheet tab and select "View Code". In the Visual Basic Editor, paste the code into the Worksheet module for the sheet you want to check for duplicates in columns A and D.
Glad to hear it! If you have any more questions or need further assistance, feel free to ask.
